title: Understanding the Virtual DOM
description: How React updates the UI efficiently.

slides.title: The Virtual DOM 🖥️
slides.content:
# Why React is Fast

React keeps a lightweight copy of the real DOM in memory – that's the **Virtual DOM**.

When you change something in your component:
1. React updates the Virtual DOM first (super fast).
2. It compares the new Virtual DOM with the previous one (a process called **diffing**).
3. It calculates the most efficient way to update the real DOM and applies **only those changes**.

This avoids expensive direct manipulation of the browser's DOM and makes your app feel snappy.

quizData.question: React updates the real DOM immediately every time state changes.
quizData.explanation: False. React first updates the Virtual DOM, then compares, and finally applies only the necessary changes to the real DOM.
